title: 移民加拿大的絕望與無力

replies.msg:
到到都開始落閘

保安局去年估算港人移民數字，估計2015年有7,000人移民外國，當中排首3位的美國、澳洲及加拿大最受歡迎。移民顧問關景鴻表示，傳統移民國家如英國及加拿大門檻較高。

TOPick先作加拿大移民簡介：

港人移居加拿大機會愈來愈渺茫，由2014年起加拿大取消多倫多、温哥華投資移民和創業移民，港人要移民加國只有循家人團聚、個別省份提名及技術移民三種。

關景鴻指，目前最易移民到加拿大的方法是，透過個別省份的投資移民計劃，以魁北克省為例，有以下3個門檻：

1. 申請人需證明有160萬加元(折合約925萬港元)資產，包括物業、股票及現金，但半年內由家人轉贈的資產不作計算；

2. 在過去5年的2年內，必須有全面的管理經驗，包括公司策劃、管理以及財務和人事管理經驗；

3. 申請人要投資80萬加元(折合約464萬港元)於政府的移民基金，申請人須向基金公司貸款，並一次性收取22萬加元(折合約127.6萬港元)作為利息，有關款項不作歸還。

但有夫婦月入10萬元，還有4個物業收租（有按揭），丈夫為IT經理、曾到加拿大及英國升學，太太任助理經理，最終被拒門外。

死因有以下各點：

1. 加拿大所指的管理經驗包括營運、人事和財務的決策權，缺一不可。職級最好是主席、CEO、CFO、Director和GM，公務員管理經驗不作考慮。

2. 該夫婦只有人事決策權，未有權開出公司抬頭支票、或部門開支預算。

3. 申請人的資產最好有80%證明由自己能力賺取回來，不包括由父母或家人贈予的現金或物業。該夫婦的物業首期由父母贈予，將租金收入用作供款。

由於該夫婦在人事及資產方面存在不明朗因素，申請人未必能得到魁北克省政府發出的提名資格。

除了投資移民，關景鴻指，申請人可透過加拿大技術移民，但方法並不容易。根據目前計分制，如申請人具大學畢業、英語流利及擁若干工作經驗，都無法達到最終門檻，除非獲當地僱主聘用及省政府提名，否則難以透過技術移民到加國。

英澳投資移民門檻高

除了加拿大，澳洲投資移民門檻更高，申請人須投資500萬澳幣(約2,959萬港元)選擇當地上市公司的股票或債劵等，為期4年，每年須有40天留在當地或4年共有180天，可取得居留權。單以投資金額而言，關景鴻指澳洲金額可算是最高。

至於英國，投資簽證Tier 1 （Investor）投資金額亦需200萬英鎊(約1,940萬港元)，成功入籍英國約需5至6年時間。

美國投資50萬美元

關景鴻指，較多港人選擇「EB5」企業投資移民，需投資50萬美元（約388萬港元）的項目，如餐館及護老院等，有關項目需在2年內維持10個就業機會。

技術移民方面，關景鴻指全世界都有人想去美國，配額不多，競爭激烈，而家屬團聚則要等候多時，即使是兄弟姊妹也需等待10年或以上。

我住魁省
本身算係最容易移民既，因為有第條隊
但黎緊十月一號大選，好大機會贏嗰個新政黨（CAQ）已經講明會改革移民政策
所有新移民都要識法文先至可以入黎
同埋取消個投資移民

但本身就算無改移民政策，唔識法文的話，你唔係做 IT 或者偏門啲既 engine (e.g. aerospace)
基本上都唔洗諗搵工呢樣嘢
唔會搵唔到既，你識法文咪搵到囉
餐館都或者搵到既，但呢度餐館都要識法文，可能又比老世玩
就算你响做 Bombardier aeropsace 你都要做"徒弟"
因為你要考 engine 牌，engineer 係 protected title，考牌要考埋你法文
無 engine 牌等比人壓榨

呢度啲樓算平，平既低過一球港紙都有，屋就可能球半到
你肯學法文又肯辛苦肯捱都應該過到
唔想學想 hea 好似篇新聞個夫婦比錢就算就開始好難

quote.msg:
我住魁省
本身算係最容易移民既，因為有第條隊
但黎緊十月一號大選，好大機會贏嗰個新政黨（CAQ）已經講明會改革移民政策
所有新移民都要識法文先至可以入黎
同埋取消個投資移民

但本身就算無改移民政策，唔識法文的話，你唔係做 IT 或者偏門啲既 engine (e.g. aerospace)
基本上都唔洗諗搵工呢樣嘢
唔會搵唔到既，你識法文咪搵到囉
餐館都或者搵到既，但呢度餐館都要識法文，可能又比老世玩
就算你响做 Bombardier aeropsace 你都要做"徒弟"
因為你要考 engine 牌，engineer 係 protected title，考牌要考埋你法文
無 engine 牌等比人壓榨

呢度啲樓算平，平既低過一球港紙都有，屋就可能球半到
你肯學法文又肯辛苦肯捱都應該過到
唔想學想 hea 好似篇新聞個夫婦比錢就算就開始好難
好鍾意呢種政策，起碼篩走唔少劣質人士

岩啦，唔識法文同英文嘅低質人口就咪去啦
利申一大工程佬，大學已經學法文

188A係比有最少兩年經商經驗既申請人黎澳洲再做兩年生意黎轉PR。要求係run個最少30%股份生意最少兩年最少50萬澳幣營業額同最少80萬總資產。

188B係比有最少3-5年炒股或者炒樓既申請人，最少澳幣2.25m總資產同澳幣1.5m投資資產。攞左188B後黎澳洲累積住2年，4年左右轉PR。

此外，呢兩種都係打分制，最少65分。

replies.msg:
Singapore is difficult to get passport, I know an Indian guy that scored top 2% and still go rejected, they currently only favour Malaysian and/or Indonesian.

And PR from Singapore is also not a good thing, first you have to actually live there (some fixed amount of days of residency per year) to retain the status; second they actually want to kick you out, I heard stories where only the guy gets the PR but not his wife, which eventually the guy would leave too.

Germany takes 8 years in total to obtain citizenship via naturalization.

http://www.bamf.de/EN/Willkommen/Einbuergerung/InDeutschland/indeutschland-node.html

For technical immigrants, I'd say at the moment the easiest is really UK or Australia, which takes 5 and 4 respectively, and less language barrier.

Investment is another story.

replies.msg:
Amsterdam is fine, they've got this huge EDM parties event every year. Hong Kong people community is relatively small comparing to London, and just English would be enough for daily usage.

Pros:
5 yrs for citizenship (at the moment, likely to be incremented to 7)
No Dutch language prerequisite as most people here speak English
Good work life balance (except for legal sector)
Food is fresh

Cons:
Weather could be terrible for Hong Kong people
A2 level Dutch is required for naturalization exams (at the moment, likely to be raised to B2 in near future)

Depends:
Inclusiveness
LGBTQ+ blah blah blah
House price together with rent is getting more and more expensive, still way more affordable than HK

replies.msg:
d有美國passport既就叫苦連天
每年在外國做野既美籍人都要交美國海外稅再交住緊國家既稅，double pay好重皮

美國醫療食自己都食窮你，奥巴馬醫保已死。英國衰極有NHS臨到死先俾藥你，但起碼包生仔

教育0個度
美帝而家334(詳情真唔知sor) , 英國而家行左香港會考制
(舊制係GCSE, AS level再A level, GCSE fail左邊科可以俾錢淨retake 0個科邊retake邊讀lower 6 AS level 4 courses, upper 6 drop 一科，以AS level grade經UCAS報大學，等interview 同un/conditional offer)

新制係GCSE讀科多左，fail到一定程度唔夠分上A level要全年重讀，cut左AS Level 4科，用GCSE grade 報大學，之後lower 同upper 6兩年淨讀A Level考大學，A level 仲有A* grade )

所以某程度上英國教育制度係倒退緊，睇下今年GCSE幾多人叫苦連天屌個試難
但個個香港學生家長都仲係的個仔去英國讀 咪讀番你個年代既會考囉on鳩，IB香港國際學校都有點解要飛去英國老蓮咁遠去讀，都係咁貴

但美國同英國大學點都出名過加拿大，第時個仔女讀得成書個朵都響過加拿大grad既講真

治安，英國最多咪冧友同打人，槍聲我住幾年聽過一下咁多
美帝 昨日電莖先整單槍擊黎

基於以上原因我麻麻地美帝，加拿大同英國就相對ok d

I'm almost done, have been working here for 5 years already and now awaiting my integration exam results, I can then start my citizenship application, takes maybe another half a year I bet.

I basically was lucky enough to get hired from abroad as high skilled immigrant, they call it KM here.

[member]You are going to get your EU Blue card as soon as you receive your citizenship.From then on you will be able to work in all schengen countries

i guess you just need to wait and everything else will take care of itself

btw how much does it cost to do a degree in netherlands? im having two options right now . germany or netherlands

I won't need blue card if I've got my citizenship coz the rights of citizenship overrides those of blue card. And yes, I'm mostly waiting things to happen for now lol.

For a degree, TBH I might do it after I've obtained citizenship, coz there would be a 80-90% discount. BTW, both countries are well aware of the existence of BNO, so don't even try to claim yourself as EU citizens, they won't let you through.

replies.msg:
Would you mind telling us what kind of skills/ experience allow you to find a job in NL without citizenship/PR? Where do you get your bachelor’s degree? STEM field?

Some background about myself: I’m graduating this year with BEng degree (not CS) from a HK uni, have been seriously considering studying masters in Germany and learning German (a long way to go in reaching B2-C1 ). It seems NL is a good place for tech guy to go too, found some great schools near Delft & Eindhoven. So comparing NL with Germany I’m not sure which is better

Some pros/cons I can think of:

🇩🇪
Good :
-Basically Free tuition if you can reach german proficiency, a few english program also free/low cost
- More good tech Unis and programs
- Larger, multi-faceted economy (hence jobs, I guess)
- Lower living cost

Bad :
- Your life will be very hard without conversational German (B2-C1)
- long neutralisation process to get PR/citizenship

🇳🇱
Good :
- Dutch speed very good english, can live and find jobs more easily without speaking dutch
- Liberal people, good government (recent years, compared to german’s merkel govt. )
- Shorter time to get PR/citizenship

Bad :
- Paid tuition, much more expensive for non-EU students
- Smaller economy, inclined to a few sectors like semiconductors
- Relatively high cost of living

I’m planning to go next year, any suggestions and comments are highly appreciated. Thanks!

replies.msg:
In your case, I’d say best option as follows:
1) get hired directly to nl as highly skilled immigrant, check openings on indeed.nl and monsterboard.nl, aim at graduate trainee. Chance might be slim to meet 30% ruling requirement though. Total 5 years
2) study in Germany and then look for jobs in nl afterwards, this way there’s a higher chance to get into highly skilled immigrants category and higher chance of meeting 30% ruling requirement due to better qualification. Be ware of the distance restriction for 30% ruling. Total around 7 years
3) study and get hired in Germany. Total 8 years

Beware of the fact that the integration exam in Germany is more difficult (C1) at the moment comparing to nl (A2). But the Dutch authorities are proposing to advance it to (B1 to B2)

Other supplementary info:
1) Right wing parties in nl are eager to reduce the duration of 30% ruling from 8 to 5 years
2) eligibility for naturalisation is likely to be lengthened to 7 years from 5 in the near future in nl
